title: chelidonium majus subsp. laciniatum
intro: Chelidonium majus subsp. laciniatum, also known as the cut-leaved celandine, is a subspecies of the greater celandine plant (Chelidonium majus) characterized by its deeply cut or lacy leaves. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anthelmintic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine and chelidonine. The plant is believed to offer health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Chelidonium majus subsp. laciniatum can be prepared for medicinal use in various forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and ointments, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
